text: U dɔɔ nauna, kauzi, peeza, buuna ani balaa kpila maanii filaŋkeŋ. Awiarɛ u tili ŋɔ ya wa kalii lugɔ u ra bau. Ka u ra suŋŋiibuu lugɔ í lɛɛu ani bɔsɔna.”

text: Denii Rachɛl ŋɔ, “N mɛ, gilɛɛ waa na baaŋ, miŋ waa wette izi chi i see la, n na ɛɛ doŋaa.” Awia, Laban faa pigzi a tanti dia maa, ka waa u dia vuga la nawɛɛ.
translated: Rachel said, "Look, I have a sister who has two children, but I have not borne any, what shall I do?" Now, Laban took the servant to the house of his mother, and the woman told her mother everything.

text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Akai, i balii naa wàá birgi i kapɛttɛ diiri. N daŋ tɛi i himmaa biibaal, u di i kapɛttɛ maa.”
translated: Then the elder said, "Akai, your farm is very close to the path. I fear that your sheep will get into the farm."

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Aizik pɛɛzuu, “Ɛrɛɛ i ɛɛ di nau manaŋ kennei?” Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi la hinna hum labuu hiiniŋ.”
translated: Isaac asked, "What is it that you have hunted today?" Jacob said, "God gave me the animals."

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Denii Rachɛl ŋɔ u be, “Guŋwaa, gil i ni n balii Bilha chɔɔ. Aŋhinabe, u daŋ wer lul biizi tɛŋ.”
translated: Then Rachel said, "Give me children, or I'll die. Jacob became angry with her and said, "Am I in the place of God?"

text: Bii naa miŋ baŋŋɛ naa ge daŋ ɛɛ bɔr la ban ninna jaamuu. Denii ń jaa kpa kuna la miŋ dɔɔwɛ fi bɔkpaŋ di tɛu.”
translated: They said that the chief of the village had died and they were going to bury him in the night. Nobody should go outside because of the guns that would be fired to announce it to the town.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
